heroHeadline: PLA Aircraft Carrier Liaoning Transits Taiwan Strait; Japan Joins US in Freedom-of-Navigation Operations
heroSubtitle: China's aircraft carrier Liaoning (CV-16) transited the Taiwan Strait on April 20 as 5 PLA sorties and 12 PLAN vessels operated around Taiwan — the highest ship count since the Apr 11 post-KMT-summit surge. Four aircraft crossed the median line into Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ. JMSDF destroyer Ikazuchi made Taiwan Strait transit on April 17 — the first Japanese destroyer transit under PM Takaichi — prompting China to label it a 'deliberate provocation'; PLA Eastern Theater responded with East China Sea 'joint combat readiness patrols' on April 18. Taiwan's legislature broke a 230-day budget deadlock on April 16, advancing the NT$1.25T special defense budget; President Lai inspected Kaohsiung army training command on April 17. 24 days to Trump-Xi Beijing summit (May 14-15).
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, CNBC, MarineLink, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
